name: Chocolate Torte
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 teaspoons baking soda dissolved in 4 tablespoons cold water
2 packages almond slices, toasted (reserve 1/2 cup for garnish)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine 3/4 cup sugar, cocoa powder, and milk. Mix until thick and set aside to cool.
2. In another large bowl, beat Crisco with 1 1/2 cups sugar until creamy. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. Gradually add the remaining ingredients (baking soda solution, sweetened milk, sifted flour, vanilla, and salt), mixing until just combined.
3. Stir in the cocoa mixture into the Crisco batter. Pour evenly into three greased and floured round pans.
4. Bake for 45 minutes at 325°F (160°C). Allow to cool completely.
Filling Instructions:
1. Melt chocolate bars in a double boiler over low heat, stirring until smooth.
2. Remove from heat and add butter. Beat at low speed until the mixture is smooth.
3. Gently fold in toasted almond slices, reserving 1/2 cup for garnish.
4. Spread about one-third of the filling over each layer of the cooled chocolate torte.
Topping Instructions:
1. In a large bowl, whip cream, sugar, vanilla extract, and cream of tartar until stiff peaks form.
2. Gently spread the whipped cream between layers or on top of the chocolate torte, and garnish with reserved almond slices.
